title: hieracium cerinthoides subsp. vogesiacum
intro: Hieracium cerinthoides subsp. vogesiacum, also known as the Voges's hawkweed, is a plant species belonging to the dandelion family (Asteraceae) and is native to the Vosges Mountains in France and neighboring regions. The medicinal properties of Hieracium cerinthoides subsp. vogesiacum include its ability to treat various health conditions, such as skin issues and wounds, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and exhibit antioxidant properties. Hieracium cerinthoides subsp. vogesiacum can be prepared as a tincture, decoction, or infusion, allowing for various herbal preparations to be used for medicinal purposes.
